briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for July 16, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Urgent Climate Crisis: Intensifying Global Heatwaves, Wildfires, and Floods
sections.content: As record-breaking heatwaves strike various regions worldwide, Chungcheongnam-do Governor Park Soo-hyun has characterized this extreme heat as 'climate murder' and has formed a special task force to respond [3, 5]. Domestically, Yongin City is also seeing increased awareness of the severity of heatwaves and climate change, exploring their impact on daily life [6]. Internationally, Chinese cities are installing 'outdoor air conditioners' on building rooftops to combat the heatwaves [12]. Concurrently, in Canada, over 100 active wildfires have led to a severe crisis, making Toronto's air quality among the worst globally and causing smoke to reach as far as New York [113, 126, 127]. In West Africa, the likelihood of extreme rainfall due to climate change has increased fivefold, resulting in large-scale floods that displaced thousands [112, 128], and the risk of flooding along the Gulf of Guinea coast is also intensifying due to climate change and urbanization pressures [111]. These complex climate disasters clearly demonstrate the reality of the climate crisis humanity faces, emphasizing the need for immediate action [122].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Policies and Efforts Towards Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Domestically, following criticisms that the 'Climate Change Impact Assessment' system only exists on paper with inadequate post-management, legislative efforts for post-management have begun to enhance the system's effectiveness [1]. Meanwhile, the Climate Change Center held a roundtable to discuss the impact of UN resolutions on domestic industries and explore future policy directions [2]. Demands for strengthening the policy foundation for achieving carbon neutrality continue; on Constitution Day, climate organizations pointed out that a Constitutional Court decision has been neglected for over five months, urging immediate amendment of the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act [20, 24, 65, 69]. Across the country, efforts for carbon neutrality through citizen participation are active. Siheung City launched a 'Climate Citizen Network' where citizens directly propose carbon neutrality policies [16, 18, 36, 42, 43, 46, 64, 70, 72], and Goyang City also activated its second 'Carbon Neutrality Citizen Participation Group' to 본격화 citizen-led climate action [17, 34, 35, 49, 67]. Thus, Korea is making diverse efforts to accelerate the transition to a carbon-neutral society through policy improvement and expanded citizen participation.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Challenges in the New Power Market
sections.content: According to the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), renewable energy generation recorded its largest-ever increase, emphasizing the need for efforts to achieve the 78% target by 2035 [78]. Domestically, Hanwha Ocean has commenced construction of the Shinan Uii offshore wind power project, actively expanding its renewable energy business [95], and Jeonbuk Province, ranking first in new and renewable energy generation, is expanding its RE100 leading industrial complex foundation through Energy Storage Systems (ESS) [89]. The Ministry of Climate has adjusted its policy by lowering the fixed price cap for solar power generation by 5% compared to the previous year, favoring power sources with lower carbon emissions [81, 87, 99, 100, 103, 104, 105, 108]. Furthermore, a renewable energy Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) brokerage platform directly connecting companies and power generators has officially begun operations [84, 86, 102], and global companies are accelerating their transition to renewable energy, with Google signing the largest renewable energy PPA in the US [82]. However, challenges related to the power grid due to renewable energy expansion remain a significant issue, as discussions on the establishment of the Honam region semiconductor cluster raised concerns about the limitations of renewable energy and the importance of grid stabilization [74, 88]. KEPCO KDN is working to solve this challenge and stabilize the power grid by combining AI and ESS technologies [97, 101, 107].

sections.title: ⚠️ Complex Impacts of Climate Change and Challenges for Future Agriculture
sections.content: Climate change poses a severe threat to the agricultural sector, with an interactive map predicting agricultural decline due to climate change by the end of the century, raising significant concerns about food security [109, 123]. The impacts on ecosystems and human health are also intensifying; as mosquito habitats expand due to climate change, enhanced monitoring for disease prevention is essential [110]. Furthermore, some studies warn of complex threats, suggesting that climate change could double water costs for urban households or lead to a hidden increase in suicide deaths [114, 115]. Domestically, various proactive attempts are being made to address this climate crisis: Dalseong-gun is expanding aerial spraying for rice pests and diseases in preparation for abnormal weather [7], and Muju-gun is increasing the supply of 'sunshade nets' to reduce fruit tree damage [10]. Research is underway to feed 'probiotics' to bees to improve their cold resistance, opening up possibilities for protecting species vulnerable to climate change [4], and AP Tech is practicing co-prosperity with farmers in the era of climate crisis through promotional sharing of its 'Wax Plus' product [8].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, along with various domestic and international efforts to overcome it. This is a time that calls for continued attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: How global heating supercharged floods in West Africa, displacing thousands
articles.description: Adaptation to frightening new normal and reducing emissions further and faster is critical, scientists warnDozens of people drowned, hundreds had to be rescued and thousands were displaced when floods struck the coasts of west Africa last month.Now scientists have concluded that the rains that caused the floods were supercharged by climate breakdown. Global heating, they say, turned what should have been a routine weather event into a climate catastrophe. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: Flooding that impacted the coasts of West Africa last month led to dozens of fatalities by drowning, hundreds of rescues, and the displacement of thousands of individuals.
summary.cause: Global heating intensified the rainfall that triggered the floods, transforming what would have been a routine weather event into a climate catastrophe.
summary.outlook: Scientists emphasize the critical importance of adapting to this alarming new reality and implementing faster, more substantial reductions in emissions.
